chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Etiquettes of the Fasting Person, What Invalidates the Fast, and What Does Not Invalidate it

text: 1860 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ صَائِمٍ يُشْتَمُ فَيَقُولُ إِنِّي صَائِمٌ سَلاَمٌ عَلَيْكَ لاَ أَشْتِمُكَ كَمَا تَشْتِمُنِي إِلاَّ قَالَ اَلرَّبُّ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى اِسْتَجَارَ عَبْدِي بِالصَّوْمِ مِنْ شَرِّ عَبْدِي قَدْ أَجَرْتُهُ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1860 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"If a fasting servant is insulted and responds by saying, 'I am fasting; peace be upon you. I will not insult you as you have insulted me,' the Lord (azj), Blessed and Exalted, says: 'My servant has sought refuge through fasting from the evil of My servant; I have granted him protection from the Fire.'"
